biography.value:
Trina Alvere — Warlock of the Quiet Patroness

Caelith Dunivar's private advisor. Patron of the arts in Loftwick. Mentor — quietly, without announcement — to Alicia.

Description

Mid-thirties. Pale. Silver-streaked dark hair worn long and braided. Wears soft fabrics in muted greens and grays. Speaks just loudly enough to be heard; people lean in to listen. A small silver pendant in the shape of a leaf hangs at the collar. Eyes that flicker too long on shadows.

Voice

Warm, unhurried, observant. Asks gentle but precise questions. Comfortable with silence. Almost never raises her voice. When she does, it is significant.

Combat behavior — Phase 2

Trina does not fight in Phase 2. If the Strong Room or the Little Palace is breached and combat begins in her presence, she casts Misty Step or uses Misty Escape (Archfey feature) to remove herself from harm and rejoin the party once the immediate threat is gone. She has the kit to be lethal. She has not used it on a Yeomanry citizen in fourteen years and does not intend to start.

Statblock summary (CR 7, PB +3)

AC 14 (Mage Armor when prepared) · HP 70 (10d8+20) · Speed 30 ft. · Init +2 · Passive Perception 11

STR 8 (–1) · DEX 14 (+2) · CON 16 (+3) · INT 14 (+2) · WIS 12 (+1) · CHA 20 (+5)

Saves: WIS +4, CHA +8.

Skills: Arcana +5, Deception +8, History +5, Insight +4, Performance +8, Persuasion +8.

Senses: Passive Perception 11. Languages: Common, Sylvan, Infernal.

Class features (Warlock 10 — The Archfey)

  • Fey Presence (1/short rest): as an action, each creature in a 10-ft cube around Trina makes a DC 16 Wisdom save. On a failed save, target is charmed or frightened (her choice) by Trina until the end of her next turn.
  • Misty Escape (1/short rest): when Trina takes damage, as a reaction she turns invisible and teleports up to 60 ft. The invisibility lasts until she attacks, casts a spell, or is forced to make a Constitution save to maintain concentration.
  • Beguiling Defenses: immune to the charmed condition. When another creature attempts to charm her, Trina may use her reaction to attempt to turn the charm back on the caster (DC 16 Wisdom save; on a failure, charmer is charmed by Trina for 1 minute).
  • Pact Boon — Tome: bears the Book of Quiet Patronage, granting three additional cantrips of any class (currently guidance, druidcraft, light).

Eldritch Invocations

  • Agonizing Blast: Eldritch Blast adds CHA modifier (+5) to damage on each beam.
  • Beguiling Influence: proficiency in Persuasion and Deception (already reflected in the skill totals above).
  • Misty Visions: may cast silent image at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.
  • Mask of Many Faces: may cast disguise self at will.
  • Otherworldly Leap: may cast jump on herself at will.

Spellcasting (Pact Magic)

Charisma-based. Spell save DC 16. Spell attack +8.

Trina has 2 spell slots, both 5th level, recharged on a short or long rest. She casts all spells she knows at 5th level when expending a slot.

Cantrips (at will): eldritch blast, mage hand, minor illusion, prestidigitation (plus tome cantrips: guidance, druidcraft, light).

Spells known:

  • 1st: hex, faerie fire, charm person
  • 2nd: misty step, calm emotions
  • 3rd: hypnotic pattern, dispel magic
  • 4th: dimension door, greater invisibility
  • 5th: hold monster

Always Prepared (Archfey expanded list): she also has access to faerie fire, sleep, calm emotions, phantasmal force, plant growth, sending, dominate beast, greater invisibility, dominate person, seeming — these are available without occupying her known-spells slots.

Actions

  • Eldritch Blast (cantrip). Ranged Spell Attack +8, range 120 ft. Hit: 1d10+5 force damage. At Warlock 10 she fires two beams per cast; she may direct them at the same or different targets.
  • Misty Step (bonus action, 5th-level slot). Teleport up to 30 ft to an unoccupied space she can see.
  • Hex (bonus action, 5th-level slot). Curse a creature she can see within 90 ft. Target takes an extra 1d6 necrotic damage when Trina hits it with an attack. While the target is hexed, Trina also gives the target disadvantage on one ability score check (her choice). Lasts up to 24 hours (5th-level slot).
  • Stiletto (carried, never drawn in Phase 2). Melee Weapon Attack +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d4+2 piercing.

What she will offer the party in Phase 2

  • Quiet hospitality at her townhouse if the party needs a meeting place outside the Little Palace.
  • A letter of introduction to Castrian Vell, a poet she sponsors who keeps an ear in Hardby's mercantile circles.
  • One use of hold monster or dimension door, expended in the party's interest, if the party has earned her commitment by mid-phase.

What she knows but will not fully share

  • Cindren & Vhal approached her twice over the years for "small loans." She declined both. She does not know the firm's conspiracy details, but she knows their odor. She will say so only if asked directly.
  • The name Tamsin Moraven: Trina sponsored Moraven's eldest daughter at a small private school six years ago. She is concerned for the family. If asked, she pales — visibly — but speaks calmly.

Mentor relationship — Alicia

Trina recognized Alicia's pact-bond before Alicia introduced herself. In private, she offers her name and says only: "We share a quiet teacher." From then on she offers gentle support without overstating her own depth.

If Alicia seems despondent, overwhelmed, or unsure during Phase 2 — especially after the political pressure of Arthen Moll's visit, or after grief about the dead clerk Merro Gilst lands on her — Trina takes Alicia aside and:

  • Speaks plainly about the cost of being chosen by something larger than oneself.
  • Offers a small gift: a folded silk scarf with embroidered moths, charged with one use of faerie fire Alicia may release on command (no slot cost; one use; replenishes after a long rest in Trina's presence).
  • Says: "You are not less for being uncertain. The certain are the ones I worry about."

Trina does not, in Phase 2, reveal her own patron's full name (Tasha / Iggwilv) or the depth of her capability. She mentors quietly and steps back.

Source

Blooming Rot, Part 2 — Phase 2. See the Trina Alvere journal for full profile and Alicia mentor notes.

description.value: Trina's Pact-of-the-Blade weapon, summoned at will: a slim silver stiletto with a leaf-shaped pommel matching her Quiet-Patroness pendant. Uses Charisma for attack and damage. Counts as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance.

Trina's pact focus — a small silver leaf-shaped pendant on a fine chain. The mark of the Quiet Patroness (Tasha / Iggwilv as the Archfey patron). Pulses faintly when she has cast or prepared a spell within the hour. Worn at her collar at all times.

As a Bonus Action, you can conjure a pact weapon in your hand—a Simple or Martial Melee weapon of your choice with which you bond—or create a bond with a magic weapon you touch; you can’t bond with a magic weapon if someone else is attuned to it or another Warlock is bonded with it. Until the bond ends, you have proficiency with the weapon, and you can use it as a Spellcasting Focus.

Whenever you attack with the bonded weapon, you can use your Charisma modifier for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity; and you can cause the weapon to deal Necrotic, Psychic, or Radiant damage or its normal damage type.

Your bond with the weapon ends if you use this feature’s Bonus Action again, if the weapon is more than 5 feet away from you for 1 minute or more, or if you die. A conjured weapon disappears when the bond ends.

Prerequisite: Level 2+ Warlock, a Warlock Cantrip That Deals Damage

Choose one of your known Warlock cantrips that deals damage. You can add your Charisma modifier to that spell’s damage rolls.

Repeatable. You can gain this invocation more than once. Each time you do so, choose a different eligible cantrip.

Foundry Note

The Make Agonizing activity can be used to enchant a cantrip which adds ", Agonizing" to its name and includes the damage bonus.

effects.name: Agonizing Blast
effects.description: Choose one of your known Warlock cantrips that deals damage. You can add your Charisma modifier to that spell’s damage rolls.

description.value:
Prerequisite: Level 2+ Warlock

You can see normally in Dim Light and Darkness—both magical and nonmagical—within 120 feet of yourself.

Foundry Note

This feature includes an Active Effect that grants you darkvision to 120 feet.

Prerequisite: Level 2+ Warlock

You can cast @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplDisguiseSe]{Disguise Self} without expending a spell slot.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplDisguiseSe inline]

Prerequisite: Level 2+ Warlock

You can cast @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplSilentImag]{Silent Image} without expending a spell slot.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplSilentImag inline]
Trina can cast Hold Monster once per long rest as a Mystic Arcanum, without expending a spell slot.

You hurl a beam of crackling energy. Make a ranged spell attack against one creature or object in range. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 Force damage.

Cantrip Upgrade. The spell creates two beams at level 5, three beams at level 11, and four beams at level 17. You can direct the beams at the same target or at different ones. Make a separate attack roll for each beam.

[[(floor((@details.level+1)/6)+1)]]{Total Beams}

A spectral, floating hand appears at a point you choose within range. The hand lasts for the duration. The hand vanishes if it is ever more than 30 feet away from you or if you cast this spell again.

When you cast the spell, you can use the hand to manipulate an object, open an unlocked door or container, stow or retrieve an item from an open container, or pour the contents out of a vial.

As a Magic action on your later turns, you can control the hand thus again. As part of that action, you can move the hand up to 30 feet.

The hand can’t attack, activate magic items, or carry more than 10 pounds.

You create a sound or an image of an object within range that lasts for the duration. See the descriptions below for the effects of each. The illusion ends if you cast this spell again.

If a creature takes a Study action to examine the sound or image, the creature can determine that it is an illusion with a successful [[/check ability=int skill=inv dc=@attributes.spell.dc]] check against your spell save DC. If a creature discerns the illusion for what it is, the illusion becomes faint to the creature.

Sound. If you create a sound, its volume can range from a whisper to a scream. It can be your voice, someone else’s voice, a lion’s roar, a beating of drums, or any other sound you choose. The sound continues unabated throughout the duration, or you can make discrete sounds at different times before the spell ends.

Image. If you create an image of an object—such as a chair, muddy footprints, or a small chest—it must be no larger than a 5-foot Cube. The image can’t create sound, light, smell, or any other sensory effect. Physical interaction with the image reveals it to be an illusion, since things can pass through it.

activation.condition: Study Action taken by other creature

description.value:
You create a magical effect within range. Choose the effect from the options below. If you cast this spell multiple times, you can have up to three of its non-instantaneous effects active at a time.

Sensory Effect. You create an instantaneous, harmless sensory effect, such as a shower of sparks, a puff of wind, faint musical notes, or an odd odor.

Fire Play. You instantaneously light or snuff out a candle, a torch, or a small campfire.

Clean or Soil. You instantaneously clean or soil an object no larger than 1 cubic foot.

Minor Sensation. You chill, warm, or flavor up to 1 cubic foot of nonliving material for 1 hour.

Magic Mark. You make a color, a small mark, or a symbol appear on an object or a surface for 1 hour.

Minor Creation. You create a nonmagical trinket or an illusory image that can fit in your hand. It lasts until the end of your next turn. A trinket can deal no damage and has no monetary worth.

You place a curse on a creature that you can see within range. Until the spell ends, you deal an extra 1d6 Necrotic damage to the target whenever you hit it with an attack roll. Also, choose one ability when you cast the spell. The target has Disadvantage on ability checks made with the chosen ability.

If the target drops to 0 Hit Points before this spell ends, you can take a Bonus Action on a later turn to curse a new creature.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Your Concentration can last longer with a spell slot of level 2 (up to 4 hours), 3–4 (up to 8 hours), or 5+ (24 hours).

materials.value: the petrified eye of a newt

activation.condition: whenever you hit the target with an attack roll

effects.name: Hexed Strength
effects.description: The target has Disadvantage on ability checks made with the chosen ability.

effects.name: Hexed Dexterity
effects.description: The target has Disadvantage on ability checks made with the chosen ability.

effects.name: Hexed Constitution
effects.description: The target has Disadvantage on ability checks made with the chosen ability.

effects.name: Hexed Intelligence
effects.description: The target has Disadvantage on ability checks made with the chosen ability.

effects.name: Hexed Wisdom
effects.description: The target has Disadvantage on ability checks made with the chosen ability.

effects.name: Hexed Charisma
effects.description: The target has Disadvantage on ability checks made with the chosen ability.

description.value: You touch a willing creature who isn’t wearing armor. Until the spell ends, the target’s base AC becomes 13 plus its Dexterity modifier. The spell ends early if the target dons armor.

effects.name: Mage Armor
effects.description: The target's base AC becomes 13 + its Dexterity modifier.

description.value: Briefly surrounded by silvery mist, you teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

You suggest a course of activity—described in no more than 25 words—to one creature you can see within range that can hear and understand you. The suggestion must sound achievable and not involve anything that would obviously deal damage to the target or its allies. For example, you could say, “Fetch the key to the cult’s treasure vault, and give the key to me.” Or you could say, “Stop fighting, leave this library peacefully, and don’t return.”

The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Charmed condition for the duration or until you or your allies deal damage to the target. The Charmed target pursues the suggestion to the best of its ability. The suggested activity can continue for the entire duration, but if the suggested activity can be completed in a shorter time, the spell ends for the target upon completing it.

effects.name: Pursuing Suggestion
effects.description: The Charmed target pursues the suggestion to the best of its ability. The suggested activity can continue for the entire duration, but if the suggested activity can be completed in a shorter time, the spell ends for the target upon completing it.

description.value:
You create a twisting pattern of colors in a 30-foot Cube within range. The pattern appears for a moment and vanishes. Each creature in the area who can see the pattern must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the &Reference[charmed apply=false] condition for the duration. While Charmed, the creature has the &Reference[incapacitated apply=false] condition and a Speed of 0.

The spell ends for an affected creature if it takes any damage or if someone else uses an action to shake the creature out of its stupor.

effects.name: Hypnotized
effects.description:
While &Reference[charmed apply=false], the creature has the &Reference[incapacitated apply=false] condition and a Speed of 0.

The spell ends for an affected creature if it takes any damage or if someone else uses an action to shake the creature out of its stupor.

description.value: You attempt to interrupt a creature in the process of casting a spell. The creature makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the spell dissipates with no effect, and the action, Bonus Action, or Reaction used to cast it is wasted. If that spell was cast with a spell slot, the slot isn’t expended.

activation.condition: when you see a creature within range casting a spell with Verbal, Somatic, or Material components

description.value:
You teleport to a location within range. You arrive at exactly the spot desired. It can be a place you can see, one you can visualize, or one you can describe by stating distance and direction, such as “200 feet straight downward” or “300 feet upward to the northwest at a 45-degree angle.”

You can also teleport one willing creature. The creature must be within 5 feet of you when you teleport, and it teleports to a space within 5 feet of your destination space.

If you, the other creature, or both would arrive in a space occupied by a creature or completely filled by one or more objects, you and any creature traveling with you each take 4d6 Force damage, and the teleportation fails.

Choose a creature that you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Paralyzed condition for the duration. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the spell on itself on a success.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 5.

effects.name: Paralyzed
effects.description: At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the [[/save ability=wis]] save, ending the spell on itself on a success.

blooming-rot-2.pact: The Quiet Patroness (Tasha / Iggwilv presented as Archfey muse — secret in setting)
